The automatic guided vehicle can stop sa.goal: The automatic guided vehicle can stop safely when encountering obstacles.
The automatic guided vehicle can stop sa.strategy: The safety of the automatic guided vehicle can be determined by examining its obstacle detection system and braking system.

subgoals.G1.1: The obstacle detection system is reliable.

The obstacle detection system is reliabl.goal: The obstacle detection system is reliable.
The obstacle detection system is reliabl.strategy: The reliability of the obstacle detection system can be determined by examining its hardware and software components.

subgoals.G1.2: The object recognition algorithm is effective.
subgoals.G1.3: The system can detect obstacles in real-time.

The braking system is effective..strategy: The effectiveness of the braking system can be determined by examining its components and performance.

subgoals.G1.1: The brake pads have sufficient friction.
subgoals.G1.2: The brake fluid is at the correct level.

The automatic guided trolley can stop sa.goal: The self-driving car is safe to operate on public roads.
The automatic guided trolley can stop sa.strategy: The safety of the self-driving car can be determined by examining its hardware and software components.

The sensor system is reliable..strategy: The reliability of the sensor system can be determined by examining its hardware and software components.

solutions.Sn1.1: Manufacturer test reports, third-party evaluations of hardware components.
solutions.Sn1.2: Code audits, simulation results for software reliability.
solutions.Sn1.3: Calibration records, independent assessments of sensor accuracy.

The navigation algorithm is accurate..strategy: The accuracy of the navigation algorithm can be determined by examining its ability to provide correct routes and detect obstacles.

subgoals.G1.1: The algorithm provides correct routes in 95% of cases.
subgoals.G1.2: The algorithm detects obstacles with a false negative rate of less than 5%.

solutions.Sn1.1: Simulation results, test drives on various routes.
solutions.Sn1.2: Sensor data analysis, obstacle detection logs.

The emergency systems function correctly.strategy: The correctness of the emergency systems can be determined by examining their individual components and their interactions.

subgoals.G1.1: The fire suppression system functions correctly.
subgoals.G1.2: The emergency power supply system functions correctly.
subgoals.G1.3: The alarm and notification system functions correctly.

solutions.Sn1.1: Test reports, third-party evaluations of the fire suppression system.
solutions.Sn1.2: Manufacturer documentation, independent assessments of the emergency power supply system.
solutions.Sn1.3: System logs, simulation results demonstrating correct alarm and notification functionality.

The hardware components are fault-tolera.strategy: The fault tolerance of the hardware components can be determined by examining their redundancy and fail-safe mechanisms.

subgoals.G1.1: The redundant systems are properly configured.
subgoals.G1.2: The fail-safe mechanisms are correctly implemented.

The 1553B bus transmits messages that co.goal: The 1553B bus transmits messages that conform to requirements.
The 1553B bus transmits messages that co.strategy: From two aspects: message transmission timing and content.

subgoals.G1.1: The 1553B bus transmits messages within the required timing constraints.
subgoals.G1.2: The 1553B bus transmits messages with correct content.

solutions.Sn1.1: Bus protocol specifications, testing results showing timing compliance.
solutions.Sn1.2: Message format documentation, validation of message contents through testing or simulation.
